name: Teller Open Banking API
what_it_does: Open banking API providing real-time read access to US bank accounts including transactions, balances, and account details. Developer-friendly Plaid alternative with flat-fee pricing and instant connectivity.
use_cases: Reading bank account balances and transactions for personal finance agents
Verifying bank account ownership for ACH payment initiation
Aggregating multi-bank financial data for wealth management applications
Income verification by analyzing transaction history programmatically
Cash flow analysis for lending decision automation agents
not_for: International bank account access (Teller is US-only)
Credit score or credit reporting data
Organizations needing established Plaid ecosystem integrations
best_when: An agent needs real-time US bank account data with developer-friendly APIs and predictable flat-rate pricing instead of Plaid's usage-based model.
avoid_when: You need global bank coverage or established vendor relationships — use Plaid or Yodlee.

auth.notes: HTTP Basic Auth using enrollment ID as username and access token as password. Users connect bank accounts via Teller Connect (embedded widget). Access tokens per enrollment with bank-issued credentials.

pricing.paid_tiers: Production: flat $10/month per enrolled account (no per-call fees)
pricing.notes: Flat per-account monthly fee — predictable pricing unlike Plaid's per-call model. Development environment free with test bank accounts. No minimum contract. Simple pricing model.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Clean, developer-friendly REST API documentation. Error responses are JSON with descriptive codes. Webhook delivery for account updates and alerts. Test bank accounts available for development.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read operations are inherently idempotent. Transaction list queries are consistent for same time range.
known_agent_gotchas: US banks only — no international account support
Account access depends on bank connectivity — some banks may require re-authentication
Teller Connect (frontend widget) required for initial bank account linking — not fully API-only
Real-time transaction data availability depends on bank — some banks have 24-hour lag

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II and PCI-DSS compliant. TLS enforced. Bank credential abstraction — Teller uses bank-issued tokens, not stored passwords. Financial data encryption at rest. US-only data residency.
